name: AA-Omniscience Hallucination Rate
description: AA-Omniscience Hallucination Rate (lower is better) measures how often the model answers incorrectly when it should have refused or admitted to not knowing the answer. It is defined as the proportion of incorrect answers out of all non-correct responses, i.e. incorrect / (incorrect + partial answers + not attempted) · Evaluation results measured independently by Artificial Analysis
measurementTechnique: Independent test run by Artificial Analysis on dedicated hardware.
citation: Artificial Analysis (2025). LLM benchmarks dataset. https://artificialanalysis.ai

mainEntity.name: Which is the most intelligent AI model?

acceptedAnswer.text: Claude Fable 5 (Adaptive Reasoning, Max Effort, Opus 4.8 Fallback) currently leads the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index with a score of 60, out of 140 models evaluated.

mainEntity.name: What are the top AI models?

acceptedAnswer.text: The top AI models by Intelligence Index are: 1. Claude Fable 5 (Adaptive Reasoning, Max Effort, Opus 4.8 Fallback) (60), 2. GPT-5.6 Sol (max) (59), 3. GPT-5.6 Sol (xhigh) (58), 4. GPT-5.6 Sol (high) (56), 5. Claude Opus 4.8 (Adaptive Reasoning, Max Effort) (56).

mainEntity.name: Which is the fastest AI model?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mercury 2 is the fastest at 782.8 tokens per second, followed by LFM2.5-VL-1.6B (431.1 t/s) and Step 3.7 Flash (390.8 t/s).

mainEntity.name: Which is the cheapest AI model?

acceptedAnswer.text: Gemma 3n E4B Instruct is the most affordable at $0.02 per 1M tokens (blended), followed by Nova Micro ($0.03) and Sarvam 30B (high) ($0.03).

mainEntity.name: Which AI model has the lowest latency?

acceptedAnswer.text: North Mini Code has the lowest time to first token at 0.32s, followed by Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite (Non-reasoning) (0.36s) and Command A+ (0.41s).

mainEntity.name: Which is the best open weights AI model?

acceptedAnswer.text: GLM-5.2 (max) is the highest-ranked open weights model with an Intelligence Index score of 51. There are 75 open weights models out of 140 total evaluated.

mainEntity.name: What are the top open weights AI models?

acceptedAnswer.text: The top open weights AI models by Intelligence Index are: 1. GLM-5.2 (max) (51), 2. MiniMax-M3 (44), 3. DeepSeek V4 Pro (Reasoning, Max Effort) (44).

mainEntity.name: Which is the best reasoning model?

acceptedAnswer.text: Claude Fable 5 (Adaptive Reasoning, Max Effort, Opus 4.8 Fallback) leads among 99 reasoning models with an Intelligence Index score of 60. Reasoning models use extended thinking to work through complex problems before providing answers.

mainEntity.name: How are AI models compared on Artificial Analysis?

acceptedAnswer.text: Models are compared across multiple dimensions including intelligence (quality), pricing, output speed (tokens per second), latency (time to first token), end-to-end response time, and context window size. Performance metrics are measured directly using standardized prompts across 572 models.

mainEntity.name: How do I compare a specific model against others?

acceptedAnswer.text: Click on any model name or row in the charts to view its dedicated page with detailed metrics and direct comparisons against similar models. You can also use the model selector to customize which models appear in each chart.
